name: Bora Bora Pearl Beach Resort & Spa

content.verdict: Pearl Beach is the boutique, adults-skewing option among Bora Bora's five-star motu resorts — 50 bungalows instead of 100+, which completely changes the vibe. Set on Motu Tevairoa on the northern edge of the lagoon, it has a long soft-sand beach facing Mount Otemanu and one of the most consistent sunset views on the island. The overwater bungalows are the classic thatched-roof Polynesian design, each with a private deck, glass floor panel and a lagoon ladder; interiors feel warmer and more authentically Polynesian than the chain hotels. The Manea Spa (literally 'massage' in Tahitian) is the most-awarded spa on the island and the couples' cabana over the water is what honeymoons are made of. The resort skews heavily couples — you will not find the kids-club energy of Conrad or Hilton here, even though it is technically not adults-only. Dining at Tevairoa (French) and Tipanié (Polynesian-international) is reliably good without being a headline attraction. Service is the standout — small-resort attention where staff remember your names by night two. Ideal for couples who want proper luxury and the full motu-island fantasy but do not want to feel like one of 200 couples in a large resort.
content.best_room: The End-of-Pontoon Overwater Bungalow is the room to book — farthest from the motu, deepest water under the deck, cleanest Otemanu view. Ask specifically for the sunset side. The Beach Suite Bungalow with private pool is an excellent alternative for couples who prefer privacy and want to mix beachfront with overwater (consider splitting the stay: 3 nights beach, 4 nights overwater).
caveats: The resort has a smaller main pool than Hilton or Conrad — not a property to choose for pool days.
Some older overwater bungalows are scheduled for refresh; ask about renovated vs. original when booking.
Food pricing is aggressive even for Bora Bora; the half-board plan is worth it.
Reef shoes recommended for the beach swim area — some coral rubble.
Kids are allowed but rarely present; do not expect a kids' club.

faqs.q: Is Bora Bora Pearl Beach adults-only?
faqs.a: No, but it skews heavily couples and honeymooners because of the bungalow-heavy layout and absence of a kids' club. In practice it functions as a semi-adults-only property most months.

faqs.q: How does it compare to Conrad Bora Bora Nui or Four Seasons?
faqs.a: Smaller, more intimate, better price than Four Seasons. The hardware is slightly older than the ultra-luxury players, but service and setting are excellent. If you want boutique over big, this is the pick.

faqs.q: Is Manea Spa really that good?
faqs.a: Yes — repeatedly named among the best spa experiences in French Polynesia. The overwater couples' cabana is the reason to book ahead.

faqs.q: What are the overwater bungalows like?
faqs.a: Classic Polynesian thatched roofs, one bedroom, bathroom with rain shower, outdoor deck with stairs to lagoon, glass floor panel in the living area. Not ultra-modern but deeply romantic.
No — Pearl Resorts is an independent Polynesian group, not part of a major loyalty chain. Book direct or via a Virtuoso/Signature travel advisor for amenities (resort credit, upgrades).
May through October is dry season and peak. September-October tends to have the best combination of weather, availability and pricing. November-April is wet and humid.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking — Bora Bora Pearl Beach Resort, [dates]

Hi reservations,

We would like to book an End-of-Pontoon Overwater Bungalow (sunset side) for 7 nights arriving [date], for our honeymoon.

Could you please confirm:
- Availability and best honeymoon rate, including any stay-longer promotions
- Confirmation the bungalow has been refreshed/renovated
- Round-trip boat transfer cost from Motu Mute airport
- Honeymoon package inclusions (champagne, canoe breakfast, turndown)
- Half-board dining plan rate
- Availability for a Manea Spa couples' signature ritual (overwater cabana) on day 4
- A private beach dinner on day 6

Thank you,
[Names]

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrive via resort boat transfer from Motu Mute (20 minutes)
